diagrams.title: Forest Ecosystem Structure - Vertical Stratification
diagrams.description: Shows the five distinct layers of a tropical rainforest from emergent layer to forest floor, with characteristic organisms and environmental conditions at each level.
elements: Emergent layer: tallest trees (30-50m), eagles, bats
Canopy layer: continuous roof, 90% forest biodiversity, 20-30m height
Understory: dim light, young trees, snakes, insects
Shrub layer: ferns, small shrubs, 2-5m height
diagrams.purpose: Understanding spatial organization and habitat differentiation in forest ecosystems, crucial for biodiversity conservation and ecological studies.

diagrams.title: Forest Food Web - Trophic Structure
diagrams.description: Illustrates energy flow through a forest ecosystem showing interconnected food chains from producers to decomposers.
elements: Tertiary Consumers: hawk, wolf, big cats
Energy loss labels at each transfer
diagrams.purpose: Understanding ecological relationships, energy transfer efficiency (10% rule), and the importance of each trophic level in maintaining ecosystem stability.

diagrams.title: Carbon Cycle in Forest Ecosystems
diagrams.description: Shows how forests act as carbon sinks through photosynthesis and storage, and the impact of deforestation on carbon emissions.
elements: Carbon storage in biomass (trunk, branches, roots)
Comparison: intact forest vs degraded forest carbon flux
diagrams.purpose: Understanding forests' role in climate regulation, carbon sequestration processes, and the climate impact of deforestation.
